description: Home Assistant custom integration for recurring task/chore tracking

tasks.name: Project Scaffold
tasks.description: Create custom_components/recurring_todos/ directory structure with manifest.json (domain, name, version, dependencies including python-dateutil, iot_class, integration_type), __init__.py (module docstring), and const.py (DOMAIN, PLATFORMS, storage keys).

tasks.name: Data Model
tasks.description: Define TaskItem dataclass in model.py with fields: uid (str, uuid4), name (str), description (str|None), status (TodoItemStatus), due_date (date|None), rrule (str|None, iCal RRULE for recurring tasks, None for one-off), completion_history (list of dicts with completed_at datetime and completed_by str|None), created_at (datetime). Include serialization to/from dict for storage.

tasks.name: Storage Layer
tasks.description: Implement store.py using homeassistant.helpers.storage.Store for .storage/recurring_todos persistence. Storage version 1 with migration support. Methods: async_load, async_save, async_add_item, async_update_item, async_remove_item. Data keyed by config entry ID to support multiple task lists.

tasks.name: Config Flow
tasks.description: Implement config_flow.py using SchemaConfigFlowHandler. User step: single text input for list name (e.g. 'Household Chores'). Creates one config entry per task list. Add strings.json and translations/en.json with flow step titles, field labels, and error messages.

tasks.name: Integration Setup
tasks.description: Implement async_setup_entry and async_unload_entry in __init__.py. Initialize store, forward setup to todo platform. Store reference in hass.data[DOMAIN][entry.entry_id] (DOMAIN = recurring_todos). Handle config entry updates and removal with proper cleanup.

tasks.name: Todo Entity
tasks.description: Implement todo.py extending TodoListEntity. Set supported_features to CREATE_TODO_ITEM | UPDATE_TODO_ITEM | DELETE_TODO_ITEM. Implement async_create_todo_item, async_update_todo_item, async_delete_todo_items. Map TaskItem model to TodoItem for the todo_items property. Entity name from config entry title.

tasks.name: Recurrence Engine
tasks.description: Implement recurrence.py using python-dateutil rrulestr parser. Function calculate_next_due(rrule_string, last_completed_date) returns next occurrence date. When async_update_todo_item detects status change to COMPLETED on a recurring task: append {completed_at, completed_by} to completion_history (full history, never pruned), calculate next due date from RRULE, reset status to NEEDS_ACTION with new due date. One-off tasks just stay completed.

tasks.name: Overdue Detection
tasks.description: Add is_overdue computed property to TaskItem (due_date < today and status != COMPLETED). Add extra_state_attributes on todo entity exposing overdue_count and overdue_tasks list. Fire recurring_todos_overdue event via hass.bus.async_fire when tasks become overdue, usable as automation trigger.

tasks.name: Custom Services
tasks.description: Register HA services in __init__.py: recurring_todos.complete_task (mark task done, trigger recurrence), recurring_todos.snooze_task (push due date forward by N days). Define service schemas in services.yaml with entity_id and task_uid selectors. No assign service yet (future feature).

tasks.name: Options Flow
tasks.description: Add OptionsFlowHandler to config_flow.py for editing list settings after creation. Options: default_recurrence (RRULE template), notification_lead_time_hours (int, how far before due date to notify), overdue_reminder_interval_hours (int, how often to re-notify for overdue tasks), notify_devices (multi-select of available mobile_app notify services).

tasks.name: Lovelace Card
tasks.description: Custom Lovelace card in JS (recurring-todos-card.js). Features: task list with due dates and overdue highlighting, add/edit task form with recurrence UI (frequency dropdown: none/daily/weekly/monthly/yearly, interval number input, day-of-week multi-select for weekly), generates RRULE string from UI selections, calls recurring_todos services. Completion history view per task. Register as custom card resource.

tasks.name: Notification Engine
tasks.description: Implement notify.py with periodic checker using async_track_time_interval (every 30 min). Checks all task lists for due/overdue tasks. Sends push notifications via notify.mobile_app_* services to devices configured in options flow. Notification content includes task name, due date, and how overdue. Respects lead_time and reminder_interval from options. Tracks last_notified per task to avoid spam.

tasks.name: HACS Packaging
tasks.description: Add hacs.json with name, render_readme, homeassistant minimum version. Add info.md for HACS integration page. Ensure manifest.json has version field. Structure repo for HACS default repository submission.

tasks.name: Backend Tests
tasks.description: Unit tests in tests/ directory: test_recurrence.py (RRULE parsing, next-due calculation for daily/weekly/monthly/custom patterns, edge cases), test_store.py (CRUD operations, persistence, migration), test_todo.py (entity creation, task lifecycle, completion with recurrence), test_config_flow.py (user step, options flow), test_notify.py (notification scheduling, device selection, spam prevention).

tasks.name: Lovelace Card Tests
tasks.description: Basic tests for Lovelace card: RRULE generation from UI selections (frequency + interval + day selections produce correct RRULE strings), card renders task list, overdue tasks highlighted. Can use simple JS test runner.

tasks.name: Architecture Documentation
tasks.description: Create docs/architecture.md: high-level overview of integration structure, module responsibilities (store.py, recurrence.py, todo.py, notify.py, config_flow.py), data flow diagrams (task creation, completion with recurrence, notification cycle), storage schema, and key design decisions. Written for LLM consumption - concise, structured, no prose filler. Include file-to-purpose mapping so an LLM can skip reading source files.

tasks.name: API Reference Documentation
tasks.description: Create docs/api-reference.md: all services with parameter schemas and examples, entity attributes and their types, events fired and their payloads, storage format with version, TaskItem model fields and types, RRULE examples for common patterns (daily, weekdays, biweekly, monthly 1st, quarterly). Structured as quick-lookup tables. LLM can answer user questions from this without reading code.

tasks.name: CLAUDE.md Project Guide
tasks.description: Create CLAUDE.md in project root: project purpose, directory layout, how to run tests, how to install for development, coding conventions used, key dependencies and why. This is the entry point an LLM reads first before any code. Keep under 100 lines.
